metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_943
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Loss of Teneurin-a reduces the apposition of active zones and neurotransmitter clusters at Drosophila neuromuscular junctions as show in Fig. 2.
Fig.3a,c shows that Teneurin-a loss results in microtubule disorganization at the boutons of Drosophila neuromuscular junctions.
In agreement with a presynaptic effect, Fig.1 shows that Teneurin-a is presynaptically localized at Drosophila NMJs.

6/11/2017 Pim
- Teneurins are transsynaptic adhesion molecules that intracellularly couple to various components of the cytoskeleton.. Experimental description: "Details of Drosophila stocks can be found in Methods."
